title: Rich Roasted Uruguayan Bean Casserole
description: A buttery, crunchy, kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring baked beans, perfect for a slow-cook dinner, and easily made dairy-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: canned beans
ingredients.notes: kidney or pinto beans work well

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked onion and garlic, canned beans, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes and melted vegan butter until well combined.
Transfer the bean mixture to a wooden board or 9x13 inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the bread crumbs evenly over the top of the bean mixture.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until the bread crumbs are golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
